jokes.text: HEY, DON'T FORGET ME. FOR WHAT? IT'S BRING YOUR DAUGHTER TO WORK DAY. AND UNLESS YOU HAD A DAUGHTER I DON'T KNOW ABOUT, I'M YOUR GIRL.
jokes.setup: Annual tradition of George Michael pretending to be Michael's daughter
jokes.punchline: The casual way George Michael delivers this gender-bending request
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven humor that establishes a running gag. George Michael's earnest delivery makes the absurd premise work.

jokes.text: WELL, SHE'S NOT MY DAUGHTER, BUT IT'S ABOUT AS CLOSE AS I'M GONNA GET. I'M A GOOD LITTLE GIRL.
jokes.setup: Narrator reveals this joke started when George Michael was 6
jokes.punchline: George Michael's commitment to the bit as a teenager makes it uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: The cringe comes from the narrator's observation that this 'joke' has aged poorly, creating layered discomfort.

jokes.text: THEY'RE OUT OF SANITARY NAPKINS IN THE LADIES' WASHROOM.
jokes.setup: George Michael is playing his 'daughter' role at the office
jokes.punchline: He reports on feminine hygiene products with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor from George Michael's commitment to an inappropriate role.

jokes.text: WEIRD KID.
jokes.setup: George Michael's sanitary napkin comment
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement from confused office worker
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. Economy of language works well.

jokes.text: PRETTY SOON I'M GOING TO HAVE TO START WEARING A BRA. RIGHT, DAD?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to end the daughter charade
jokes.punchline: George Michael escalates to even more uncomfortable territory
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the discomfort. George Michael's obliviousness to appropriateness is well-crafted.

jokes.text: I CANNOT KILL THIS JOKE.
jokes.setup: Michael's resistance to continuing the tradition
jokes.punchline: George Michael's meta-awareness that he's committed to this bit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor. George Michael acknowledging it's a 'joke' while being unable to let it go is perfect character work.

jokes.text: WELL, EXCUSE ME, MICHAEL, FOR NOT RAISING THE PERFECT DAUGHTER THAT YOU DID.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing Tobias and Lindsay's parenting through Maeby watching TV
jokes.punchline: Lindsay sarcastically praising Michael's 'daughter' - who is actually his son
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony that works on multiple levels - the gender confusion and the parenting critique.

jokes.text: AS A PSYCHOTHERAPIST, TOBIAS WAS ON THE FOREFRONT OF THE SELF-ESTEEM MOVEMENT.
jokes.setup: Tobias indulging Maeby watching TV all day
jokes.punchline: Narrator's dry observation about Tobias's professional justification for lazy parenting
jokes.explanation: Classic Arrested Development narrator irony. The professional terminology contrasts with the obvious neglect.

jokes.text: HOMOSEXUAL. MAEBY, PLEASE.
jokes.setup: Tobias describing feelings about longing and pull to unnamed patient
jokes.punchline: Maeby casually finishing his obvious psychological assessment while he protests
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Maeby's bluntness cuts through Tobias's professional deflection.

jokes.text: SHE'S RIGHT THOUGH. YOU PROBABLY ARE A HOMOSEXUAL.
jokes.setup: Maeby's blunt assessment of Tobias
jokes.punchline: Lindsay matter-of-factly agreeing with her daughter
jokes.explanation: Good escalation. Lindsay's casual agreement amplifies the awkwardness for Tobias.

jokes.text: OH, BLESS HER. IT'S LIKE SHE KNEW WHAT I WAS GOING TO DO NEXT.
jokes.setup: Maeby asking what she'll do this year - watch him watch TV
jokes.punchline: Tobias treating this as prescient rather than pathetically predictable
jokes.explanation: Tobias's obliviousness to his own predictability is well-characterized.

jokes.text: GIRLS WITH LOW SELF-ESTEEM.
jokes.setup: Gob's magic show filmed for a 'popular video series'
jokes.punchline: The series title reveals the exploitative nature
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect economy. The title subverts expectations about 'spring break videos.'

jokes.text: NOT REALLY. KIDS LOVE BOUNDARIES.
jokes.setup: Tobias warning that Maeby doesn't respond well to strict directives, then she immediately complies
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry contradiction of Tobias's parenting theory
jokes.explanation: Smart setup/payoff that exposes Tobias's pseudo-psychology while showing Michael's practical parenting.

jokes.text: THE ONLY THING THIS GUY COULD MAKE FLY AWAY WAS THE CROWD. DOUCHE CHILL.
jokes.setup: Gob's magic performance being critiqued on TV
jokes.punchline: Brutal one-liner followed by the host's physical reaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent roast-style joke with great structure. 'Douche chill' as physical reaction adds impact.

jokes.text: YEAH, WELL, IF YOU GIVE SOMEONE PERMISSION TO USE A TISSUE, YOU CAN'T BE UPSET IF THEY BLOW THEIR NOSE.
jokes.setup: Michael angry about Gob destroying the yacht he gave permission to use
jokes.punchline: Gob's completely illogical analogy comparing yacht destruction to tissue use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - completely nonsensical but delivered with total confidence. The analogy is hilariously inappropriate.

jokes.text: ONLY TWO OF THOSE WORDS DESCRIBE MOM, SO I KNOW YOU'RE LYING TO ME.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claiming the outfit is from 'sweet old thing' mom
jokes.punchline: Michael's mathematical breakdown of which adjectives actually apply to Lucille
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical approach to calling out the lie. Works because we know Lucille is indeed neither sweet nor a thing.

jokes.text: APPLIED FOR A JOB.
jokes.setup: Lindsay went to upscale store in desperation and...
jokes.punchline: Instead of shoplifting, she did something even more beneath her - got employed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that perfectly captures Lindsay's priorities - work is more shameful than theft. Economy and character integration are perfect.

jokes.text: WELL, THEY EXPECT A CERTAIN AMOUNT OF THEFT, MICHAEL. IT'S BUILT INTO THE PRICE. IF I DIDN'T TAKE IT, THEN PEOPLE WOULD BE OVERPAYING FOR NOTHING.
jokes.setup: Michael confronting Lindsay about stealing
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's completely twisted logic that theft is actually helping customers
jokes.explanation: Classic Lindsay rationalization. The logic is completely backwards but delivered with conviction.

jokes.text: YEAH, I'VE SEEN YOUR TAPE.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he could do sleight of hand better than Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Lindsay referencing Gob's embarrassing magic performance we just saw
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier scene. Lindsay's quick wit in connecting Michael to Gob's failure works well.

jokes.text: I DON'T KNOW... MY ASS.
jokes.setup: Gob asking what Lindsay used for misdirection while shoplifting
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's honest, crude answer about her physical assets
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's blunt honesty about using her sexuality is both funny and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: MICHAEL, IT WAS SHOPLIFTING, AND I'M WHITE. I THINK I'M GOING TO BE OKAY.
jokes.setup: Michael warning Lindsay about prison like their father
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's casual acknowledgment of racial privilege in the justice system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that's both socially aware and perfectly in character. Lindsay's privilege is stated matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: THAT WAS ODD.
jokes.setup: Lindsay confidently stating Maeby won't go with Michael, then immediately being proven wrong
jokes.punchline: Michael's massive understatement about the reversal
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier moment. Michael's deadpan works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: DOUCHE CHILL.
jokes.setup: Gob showing off his purse-outfit coordination to an empty room
jokes.punchline: George Michael using the TV host's earlier phrase to describe his uncle
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows George Michael picked up the phrase. His timing and application are perfect.

jokes.text: HEY, MAEBY! MAEBY!
jokes.setup: Michael chasing after his niece
jokes.punchline: The implicit pun in calling 'Maybe' when he's unsure she'll respond
jokes.explanation: Subtle wordplay built into the character's name. Works on rewatch when you notice the pun.

jokes.text: OKAY, SO YOU CAN RUN FASTER THAN ME. BUT YOU CAN'T MAKE ME WORK.
jokes.setup: Michael chasing Maeby who clearly outran him
jokes.punchline: Maeby's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of victory plus continued defiance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Maeby. Her logical but defiant response to losing the chase is well-crafted.

jokes.text: I LIKED IT BETTER WHEN HE JUST SAID ANNYONG.
jokes.setup: Annyong delivering a complex explanation about his mother's dating needs
jokes.punchline: Michael's nostalgic preference for when Annyong was just a one-word character
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about character development. Michael expressing audience frustration with Annyong becoming more complex.

jokes.text: NO, HE'S NOT! I CAME OUT OF YOU! HE DIDN'T!
jokes.setup: Lucille calling Annyong Buster's little brother
jokes.punchline: Buster's weirdly biological and possessive protest about his birth status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - his jealousy expressed through weirdly literal biological facts. The emphasis is perfectly placed.

jokes.text: DID YOU HEAR THAT EVERYONE? MICHAEL BLUTH IS THREATENING ME!
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining Michael fired Kitty and it wasn't clean
jokes.punchline: Flashback showing Kitty's dramatic, public accusation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Kitty's dramatic tendencies. Sets up later police investigation nicely.

jokes.text: IN FACT, THERE WAS SOMEONE ELSE WHO LOOKED LIKE LUCILLE'S HUSBAND... HIS TWIN BROTHER, OSCAR.
jokes.setup: Lucille needing someone to look like her husband
jokes.punchline: The obvious solution of using George Sr.'s identical twin
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for upcoming scenes. The narrator's matter-of-fact delivery of the obvious solution works well.

jokes.text: YEAH, MAYBE A NEW PAIR OF SHOES WILL CHEER ME UP.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to prove his masculinity
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining it with stereotypically feminine shopping desires
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - completely oblivious to how his statement contradicts his masculine posturing.

jokes.text: I WOULD LIKE TO SEE IF YOU COULD GO ONE DAY WITHOUT TELLING A LIE. JUST ONE WHOLE DAY WITHOUT BEING LIKE YOUR MOM.
jokes.setup: Michael challenging Maeby's dishonesty
jokes.punchline: The devastating comparison to Lindsay (who Maeby knows is terrible)
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's truth-telling plot. Michael weaponizing Maeby's disdain for her mother is effective.

jokes.text: AND 50 BUCKS. FINE. I'LL DO IT IF YOU DO IT. THAT-A-GIRL. THIS IS GONNA BE FUN. I WIN.
jokes.setup: Michael offering money for honesty, Maeby turning it into mutual challenge
jokes.punchline: Maeby immediately claiming victory through the bet itself
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation that shows Maeby's tactical thinking. Her immediate 'I win' shows she's already planning ahead.

jokes.text: I'M ON IT. HOW CAN I HELP YOU?
jokes.setup: Police asking for Michael Bluth, daughters running around chaos
jokes.punchline: Michael's oblivious professional response to obvious police investigation
jokes.explanation: Decent visual comedy with kids running around during serious police business. Michael's corporate response is mildly funny.

jokes.text: OR AROUND TOWN OR WHATEVER.
jokes.setup: Officer saying they're investigating Kitty's disappearance, last seen 'alive'
jokes.punchline: Awkward correction that makes it sound even more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good awkward comedy from incompetent police work. The correction makes things worse.

jokes.text: NOT ALIVE. OR DEAD OR WHATEVER.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if Kitty has been seen since the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Officer's casual callback to his earlier phrasing
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that maintains the officer's casual attitude toward potential murder.

jokes.text: I CERTAINLY DIDN'T... DIDN'T KILL HER AND DROP HER IN A BACK BAY. BOY, I HOPE THAT'S NOT WHAT HAPPENED TO HER. I'M SURE IT ISN'T.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to be helpful to police investigation
jokes.punchline: His 'helpful' suggestion becomes increasingly specific and suspicious
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Michael's nervous oversharing. Each addition makes him look more guilty while he thinks he's being helpful.

jokes.text: PLEASE SEND OFFICER DAVIS AND LITTLE HANNA TO THE BACK BAY.
jokes.setup: Michael's overly specific suggestion about back bay body disposal
jokes.punchline: Officer immediately dispatching team to search that exact location
jokes.explanation: Great immediate payoff to Michael's nervous oversharing. The casual inclusion of 'little Hanna' adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: SO YOU KILLED KITTY, HUH?
jokes.setup: Michael explaining he didn't kill Kitty but has to answer questions
jokes.punchline: Maeby's casual, matter-of-fact assumption about murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby response - completely unfazed by potential murder. Her casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: AND APPARENTLY A FUN ONE.
jokes.setup: Michael insisting he's honest with no secrets
jokes.punchline: Maeby's dry assessment of his entertainment value
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan from Maeby. Her assessment of Michael as entertainment rather than role model is well-crafted.

jokes.text: I THINK THAT'S HER RIGHT BEHIND YOU THERE.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if anyone's seen Kitty lately
jokes.punchline: She's literally right there in the office
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff to the whole investigation. Simple but effective misdirection.

jokes.text: I DO KNOW STAGE COMBAT IF THE PARTNER IS WILLING AND A BIT MORE PETITE THAN I.
jokes.setup: Tobias applying for security guard position
jokes.punchline: His theatrical qualifications are completely inappropriate for actual security work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias - his theatrical background makes him hilariously unsuited for real security. The specificity of 'petite partner' is great.

jokes.text: LIKE, UH, WERE-WERE YOU EVER AWKWARD AROUND GIRLS? WHAT DO YOU MEAN? LIKE IF THERE WERE THREE OF US AND I DIDN'T KNOW WHERE TO START?
jokes.setup: George Michael asking innocent question about dating awkwardness
jokes.punchline: Gob immediately thinking about threesomes
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that shows the generational/maturity gap. Gob's misunderstanding is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: HOW MANY MICE WILL $13 BUY?
jokes.setup: GOB taking George Michael on a mysterious errand
jokes.punchline: The bizarre specificity of buying mice with exactly $13
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdity. The specificity of both the amount and the request makes it wonderfully bizarre.

jokes.text: YOU WERE... YOU WERE JUST A TURD OUT THERE, YOU KNOW? YOU COULDN'T KICK AND YOU COULDN'T RUN. YOU KNOW, YOU WERE JUST A-A TURD.
jokes.setup: Buster thinking he was banned from sports for parental reasons
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s brutally honest assessment of Buster's athletic abilities
jokes.explanation: Harsh but funny truth about Buster. George Sr.'s repetition of 'turd' and prison-influenced speech patterns work well.

jokes.text: YOU MAKE HIM WISH THAT HE'D NEVER SHOWN THAT LUDICROUS HEAD OF HAIR... AND THAT NICE FACE OF HIS.
jokes.setup: George Sr. angrily telling Buster to get rid of Oscar
jokes.punchline: His anger dissolving into admiration for Oscar's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Sr.'s conflicted feelings about his twin. The shift from anger to appreciation is well-crafted.

jokes.text: NO TOUCHING! OH! HER SELF-ESTEEM IS THROUGH THE ROOF.
jokes.setup: Father and son trying to shake hands in prison
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s positive spin on the prison's strict policies
jokes.explanation: Good callback with George Sr. finding the silver lining in prison restrictions.

jokes.text: I DIDN'T KNOW YOU HAD A DAUGHTER? I DON'T. I THOUGHT YOU WANTED ME TO CALL YOU DADDY?
jokes.setup: Police confusion about Michael having a daughter
jokes.punchline: Maeby's reference to their earlier agreement, sounding inappropriate out of context
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates new awkwardness in different context. Maeby's timing and the officer's reaction work well.

jokes.text: OOH! CHILLS. WHY DO I KEEP GETTING THESE BRUISES?
jokes.setup: Barry watching the yacht explosion footage
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate physical reaction to violence imagery
jokes.explanation: Classic Barry weirdness - his inappropriate responses and mysterious injuries are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: IF I LIE IN FRONT OF HER, IT COSTS ME 50 BUCKS. I DO THINK THAT WE SHOULD LIE.
jokes.setup: Barry advising everyone to lie, Michael explaining his honesty bet
jokes.punchline: Barry immediately contradicting his own advice when it costs Michael money
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Barry's flexible ethics. His immediate reversal when money is involved is well-crafted.

jokes.text: SAY WHAT YOU WILL ABOUT AMERICA... 13 BUCKS STILLS GETS YOU A HELL OF A LOT OF MICE. WHO SAID ANYTHING BAD ABOUT AMERICA?
jokes.setup: Gob's patriotic observation about mouse purchasing power
jokes.punchline: George Michael's confusion since no one criticized America
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob non-sequitur. His defensive patriotism about mice pricing when no one criticized anything is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: MICE! MICE! CALL SECURITY!
jokes.setup: Gob's plan to use mice for misdirection while shoplifting
jokes.punchline: The mice release creates chaos exactly as planned
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Gob's ridiculous plan. The chaos and George Michael's innocent participation work well.

jokes.text: AND A SURPRISINGLY CATLIKE ONE.
jokes.setup: Tobias now working as a security guard
jokes.punchline: Narrator's observation about his unexpected physical agility
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy implied by the narrator. Tobias being unexpectedly athletic is a nice character surprise.

jokes.text: WHAT ARE THEY GONNA TAKE, THE ONE CAN OF COMET YOU'VE GOT IN THERE?
jokes.setup: Oscar worried about his camper's security with just a hanger for a lock
jokes.punchline: Lucille's harsh assessment of his meager possessions
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille cruelty delivered with perfect timing. The specific detail about 'one can of Comet' adds to the humiliation.

jokes.text: OH, YOUR TONGUE IS AS SHARP AS YOUR BODY IS STILL TAUT.
jokes.setup: Lucille's cruel comment about Oscar's poverty
jokes.punchline: Oscar's inappropriate romantic response to being insulted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar response - turning an insult into a compliment. His romantic delusion about Lucille is well-established.

jokes.text: OSCAR, THIS IS A CHILDREN'S SOCCER GAME, NOT WOODSTOCK.
jokes.setup: Oscar behaving inappropriately at the soccer game
jokes.punchline: Lucille's sharp distinction between contexts
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast. Lucille's propriety versus Oscar's hippie background creates natural conflict.

jokes.text: NO, NO, NO. NO, NO, NO. NO! NO! NO! I CAME HERE TO SAY SOMETHING.
jokes.setup: Buster arriving to confront Oscar about his mother
jokes.punchline: His repeated 'no' building to dramatic confrontation announcement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Buster working up courage. The repetitive 'no' building to announcement is well-structured.

jokes.text: NO, SHE'S HAPPY. SHE'S JUST MEAN ALL THE TIME.
jokes.setup: Oscar suggesting Lucille isn't happy
jokes.punchline: Buster's defense that accidentally confirms Oscar's point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic fail from Buster. His attempt to defend his mother perfectly illustrates the problem. Economy and character work are excellent.

jokes.text: ARE YOU NUTS? NOT WITHOUT A BETTER LAWYER.
jokes.setup: Police asking if Michael will take a polygraph
jokes.punchline: Barry's honest assessment of his own legal abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment - accidentally admitting his incompetence while trying to give advice. Self-awareness delivered unconsciously.

jokes.text: ♪ TOO RA LOO RA LOO RA ♪
jokes.setup: Baby crying during police interrogation
jokes.punchline: Officer singing lullaby to calm daughter during serious investigation
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment mixing parenting with police work. Not exceptional but fits the episode's chaos.

jokes.text: I GIVE YOU EVIDENCE AGAINST MY FATHER, AND YOU PRETEND THAT I DIDN'T KILL KITTY? I MEAN, I-I DIDN'T KILL KITTY.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to negotiate with police
jokes.punchline: His hypothetical deal accidentally sounds like confession
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Michael's nervous oversharing pattern. His correction comes too late to help.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, THERE'S A GROVER BOOK... I CAN'T HOLD IT IN. WORKED FOR US.
jokes.setup: Michael having an emergency with his son
jokes.punchline: Officer's casual parenting advice during murder investigation
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing how parenting concerns transcend professional situations.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, THERE'S A GROVER BOOK... I CAN'T HOLD IT IN.
jokes.setup: Officer trying to help with crying baby
jokes.punchline: Professional police work interrupted by parenting advice and bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the chaos of bring-your-daughter-to-work day affecting serious police business.

jokes.text: IF YOU CAN KICK, FATTY. I'M A MAN.
jokes.setup: Kid challenging Buster to kick the ball
jokes.punchline: Buster's declaration of manhood as motivation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Buster's newfound assertiveness. His response to being called 'fatty' drives his development.

jokes.text: I WASN'T SHOPLIFTING, YOU TWIT. YES, THANKS TO ME. NO. I WORK HERE. AT LEAST I USED TO.
jokes.setup: Family argument about who's the shoplifter
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Lindsay was legitimately working, got fired because of Gob
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier misdirection. The irony that her legitimate job was ruined by Gob's actual crime works well.

jokes.text: THERE'S ONLY ONE MAGICIAN IN THIS FAMILY AND THAT IS ME. AND YOU GOT CAUGHT BY A 13-YEAR-OLD GIRL.
jokes.setup: GOB asserting his magical superiority
jokes.punchline: Immediate undermining by being caught by a teenager
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony where GOB's boasting is immediately undermined by his failure.

jokes.text: I KNEW IT! I KNEW YOU COULDN'T PULL IT OFF. THERE'S ONLY ONE MAGICIAN IN THIS FAMILY AND THAT IS ME. AND YOU GOT CAUGHT BY A 13-YEAR-OLD GIRL.
jokes.setup: Gob claiming magical superiority over Lindsay
jokes.punchline: His victory is being caught by a child while Lindsay was innocent
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Gob's misplaced pride. His boasting about being the only magician while caught by Maeby is well-crafted irony.

jokes.text: BECAUSE I'M NOT DAUGHTER ENOUGH FOR YOU.
jokes.setup: George Michael feeling hurt that Michael didn't bring him to work
jokes.punchline: His wounded use of the daughter persona shows how much it meant to him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant emotional payoff to the running gag. Shows the joke meant more to George Michael than we realized. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: I'VE PUT A BOUNTY ON MY OWN HEAD.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing he promised Maeby $50 to tell the truth to police
jokes.punchline: His recognition that he's incentivized his own downfall
jokes.explanation: Great self-awareness from Michael. The bounty metaphor perfectly captures how his good intentions backfired.

jokes.text: NO, IT'S OKAY. I'M DETECTIVE FELLOWS'S DAUGHTER. OH. OH, SORRY. IT'S FOUR-THREE-FIVE. AND THAT OPENS ANY DOOR IN THE BUILDING.
jokes.setup: Maeby lying to get into police station
jokes.punchline: Officer giving her security codes based on her fake identity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Maeby's lie resulting in major security breach. Shows her natural criminal instincts.

jokes.text: RELAX. THEY GOT NOTHING ON YOU. THEY'RE BLUFFING. KITTY'S ALIVE. I JUST SAW HER IN THE BACK ROOM.
jokes.setup: Michael confused about being released from police questioning
jokes.punchline: Maeby casually revealing she discovered the whole investigation was fake
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the investigation plot. Maeby's casual delivery of major revelation shows her unflappable nature.

jokes.text: YEAH. I'D SAVE YOUR MONEY. KITTY'S GOT A LOT OF EVIDENCE, AND SHE THINKS THAT YOU TRIED TO KILL HER. SO YOU'RE PROBABLY GOING TO NEED A BETTER ATTORNEY.
jokes.setup: Michael offering Maeby the $50 for telling the truth
jokes.punchline: Maeby's practical advice about legal defense costs being higher
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby - practical and mercenary while delivering bad news. Her financial planning around Michael's legal troubles is well-crafted.

jokes.text: LO SIENTO. I HATE BRING YOUR DAUGHTER TO WORK DAY.
jokes.setup: Michael's disastrous day trying to be a good role model
jokes.punchline: Both characters expressing regret about the tradition, Maeby in Spanish
jokes.explanation: Good ending callback that shows both characters learned something. Maeby's Spanish adds character detail.

jokes.text: LO SIENTO. I HATE BRING YOUR DAUGHTER TO WORK DAY.
jokes.setup: The entire episode's events stemming from bring your daughter to work day
jokes.punchline: Michael's defeated conclusion about the tradition
jokes.explanation: Perfect button on the episode that ties back to the opening premise while showing Michael's defeat.

jokes.text: WHERE IS EVERYBODY? HEADS UP, FATTY!
jokes.setup: Final scene showing Annyong getting revenge
jokes.punchline: Using the same insult the kid used on Buster earlier
jokes.explanation: Good final callback showing Annyong learned the insult and is using it strategically. Nice symmetry to Buster's earlier experience.
